alerts.name: 2024-alert-001
alerts.description: A tool associated with credential dumping activity (Mimikatz) was detected on the virtual machine PROD-VM-01. Attackers use these tools to harvest credentials from memory.
recommended_actions: ⚠️  IMMEDIATE: Escalate to Tier 2 — critical-path indicator detected
Rotate all credentials on PROD-VM-01
Check for lateral movement using stolen credentials
Review privileged account activity in Azure AD sign-in logs
Document findings in ServiceNow/ticketing system with this report attached
remediation_steps: Isolate the virtual machine from the network immediately
Collect volatile evidence before powering off
Rotate all credentials that may have been exposed
Review recent authentication events for lateral movement

alerts.name: 2024-alert-002
alerts.display_name: Suspicious RDP Activity from External IP
alerts.description: Suspicious Remote Desktop Protocol (RDP) activity was detected from an external IP address 185.220.101.45 to virtual machine PROD-VM-02. This IP is associated with known threat actor infrastructure.
recommended_actions: ⚠️  IMMEDIATE: Escalate to Tier 2 — critical-path indicator detected
Isolate PROD-VM-02 from internal network
Review east-west traffic in NSG flow logs
Check for additional compromised hosts via similar alerts
Block source IP 185.220.101.45 in Azure NSG and perimeter firewall
Document findings in ServiceNow/ticketing system with this report attached
remediation_steps: Block the source IP 185.220.101.45 at NSG and perimeter firewall
Review RDP access logs for successful authentication
Restrict RDP access to VPN only via Network Security Group
Enable Just-in-Time VM access in Defender for Cloud

alerts.name: 2024-alert-003
alerts.description: Behavior consistent with ransomware was detected on PROD-VM-03. Multiple files were renamed with unknown extensions and shadow copies were deleted using vssadmin.
recommended_actions: ⚠️  IMMEDIATE: Escalate to Tier 2 — critical-path indicator detected
IMMEDIATELY isolate PROD-VM-03 from all networks
Check Azure Backup for pre-infection restore points
Notify CISO and Legal within 30 minutes
Document findings in ServiceNow/ticketing system with this report attached
remediation_steps: IMMEDIATELY isolate PROD-VM-03 from all network connections
Do NOT power off — preserve volatile evidence in RAM
Check Azure Backup for pre-infection restore points
Escalate to Tier 2 / DFIR team
Notify CISO and Legal within 30 minutes

alerts.name: 2024-alert-005
alerts.description: Internal host DEV-VM-02 performed a port scan against 45 internal IP addresses, probing ports 22, 445, 3389, and 1433. This may indicate lateral movement reconnaissance.
recommended_actions: ⚠️  IMMEDIATE: Escalate to Tier 2 — critical-path indicator detected
Verify if scanning is from an authorized tool
Review NSG flow logs for follow-on exploitation attempts
Apply NSG rules to restrict east-west traffic
Block source IP 10.0.1.42 in Azure NSG and perimeter firewall
Document findings in ServiceNow/ticketing system with this report attached
remediation_steps: Investigate DEV-VM-02 for signs of compromise
Check if this is authorized scanning (vulnerability scanner IP?)
Review network flow logs for successful connections after scan
Apply NSG rules to restrict lateral east-west traffic

alerts.name: 2024-alert-004
alerts.description: A PowerShell command with obfuscated, base64-encoded content was executed on DEV-VM-01. This technique is commonly used by attackers to evade detection.
recommended_actions: Decode and analyse the executed payload
Review parent process for initial infection vector
Check recent email and downloads on the host
Document findings in ServiceNow/ticketing system with this report attached
remediation_steps: Review the decoded base64 payload to assess intent
Check parent process to determine initial infection vector
Enable PowerShell Script Block Logging
Review recent downloads and email attachments on this host

alerts.name: 2024-alert-006
alerts.description: An unusual number of Azure management operations were performed from an unfamiliar location (IP: 194.165.16.72, Country: RU). Operations included listing storage account keys and modifying NSG rules.
recommended_actions: Review Azure AD sign-in logs for this identity
Check for MFA bypass or Conditional Access policy gaps
Revoke active sessions for the compromised identity
Block source IP 194.165.16.72 in Azure NSG and perimeter firewall
Document findings in ServiceNow/ticketing system with this report attached
remediation_steps: Review Azure Activity Log for all operations from this IP
Rotate storage account access keys immediately
Revoke any tokens issued during this session
Enable Conditional Access policy requiring MFA from unfamiliar locations
Review and revert any NSG changes made during this session

alerts.name: 2024-alert-007
alerts.display_name: Multiple Failed Login Attempts on Azure VM
alerts.description: 47 failed SSH authentication attempts were detected against DEV-VM-03 from IP 45.33.32.156 within 10 minutes. Account admin was targeted.
recommended_actions: Rotate all credentials on DEV-VM-03
Check for lateral movement using stolen credentials
Review privileged account activity in Azure AD sign-in logs
Block source IP 45.33.32.156 in Azure NSG and perimeter firewall
Document findings in ServiceNow/ticketing system with this report attached
remediation_steps: Block 45.33.32.156 at NSG inbound rules
Disable password authentication for SSH — use key pairs only
Enable Just-in-Time VM access to restrict SSH exposure
Review auth.log for any successful logins from this IP
